content.text: I need to programmatically link a series of tasks so that each one depends on the previous task in the list.

To create this linear chain efficiently, we use `zip` with two slices of the input tuple. This pairs every upstream task with its corresponding downstream successor for us.

```python
def chain(*tasks):
    """
    Given a number of tasks, builds a dependency chain.

    chain(task_1, task_2, task_3, task_4)

    is equivalent to

    task_1.set_downstream(task_2)
    task_2.set_downstream(task_3)
    task_3.set_downstream(task_4)
    """
    for up_task, down_task in zip(tasks[:-1], tasks[1:]):
        up_task.set_downstream(down_task)
```

This approach relies completely on all provided objects having a defined `.set_downstream()` method to successfully establish the dependency link.
What happens if I call chain() with zero tasks or only one task?
Got it, that covers the edge cases well!
